findings.description: The PDF uses a centered, inline agenda format with dotted leaders connecting topics to presenters, while the HTML converts this into a formal table with three columns (Time, Topic, Presenter). The structural presentation is fundamentally different.
findings.evidence: PDF shows agenda items as lines with dotted leader characters connecting topic text to presenter names. HTML renders a bordered table with explicit column headers 'Time', 'Topic', and 'Presenter'.

findings.description: The title formatting differs. The PDF has 'French Broad Hydrologic Model' and 'Stakeholder Meeting #2' as centered bold text of similar size. The HTML renders 'French Broad Hydrologic Model' as a much larger heading with a horizontal rule underneath, and 'Stakeholder Meeting #2' as a separate smaller heading.
findings.evidence: PDF: Both title lines are centered bold, similar font size. HTML: 'French Broad Hydrologic Model' is significantly larger with a red/dark horizontal line separator, 'Stakeholder Meeting #2' is rendered as a distinct sub-heading.

findings.description: The location and date information is left-aligned in the HTML but centered in the PDF.
findings.evidence: PDF: 'Land of Sky Council of Governments', address, and date are all centered. HTML: These are left-aligned.

findings.description: Reading order is preserved - all content appears in the same sequence despite structural differences.
findings.evidence: Both versions present: title, meeting number, location, date, agenda items in chronological order, closing message.

findings.description: The PDF presents the agenda as a formatted list with dotted leaders, not as a table with explicit headers. The HTML converts this into a table with fabricated column headers ('Time', 'Topic', 'Presenter') that do not appear in the PDF.
findings.evidence: The PDF has no table headers. The HTML adds <th>Time</th>, <th>Topic</th>, <th>Presenter</th> which are reasonable inferences but not present in the source.

findings.description: The HTML uses h2 for 'Stakeholder Meeting #2', which is part of the same title block in the PDF. This is a reasonable structural choice but the PDF presents the title as a single centered block rather than a hierarchical heading structure.
findings.evidence:
PDF: 'French Broad Hydrologic Model / Stakeholder Meeting #2' as a single title block. HTML:

French Broad Hydrologic Model

Stakeholder Meeting #2

findings.description: The HTML has nested <main> elements - an outer <main> from the template and an inner <main> wrapping the table content. This is a structural duplication of the main element, though content is not duplicated.
findings.evidence: HTML contains <main> wrapping the entire body content, then another <main> after the <header> section.
